title: Tableau Dashboard and Sheet Layout with Usage Data and Field Drill-Down
story: As a data analyst, I want to browse Tableau workbooks and drill down into dashboards and sheets to see measures, dimensions, formulas, filters, and data sources, so that I can understand and govern my Tableau analytics assets.
description:
Mirror Tableau's navigation model in DataTools Pro: Workbook → Dashboard (with child sheets) → Sheet field details.

### Sheet Sidebar Detail
- Measures and dimensions
- Expression formulas/logic
- Filters applied
- Data source

### Dashboard View
- Aggregates all sheets and their fields
- Packaged as JSON for eventual Metrics Analyst integration

### Usage Data
- `__typename` distinguishes dashboards from sheets
- `path` identifies sheets/dashboards accessible to end users vs. internal sheets

### Navigation Structure
Workbook → Views (sheets and dashboards) → Sheet field detail sidebar

GraphQL queries retrieve workbooks with views, tags, and ownership. The `__typename` field type and `path` field can also be retrieved via REST API.
acceptance_criteria: Workbook browser shows all workbooks with views (sheets/dashboards)
Dashboards can be drilled into to see child sheets
Sheet sidebar shows measures, dimensions, formulas, filters, and data sources
Usage data and accessibility (path) are displayed
Dashboard field data can be exported as JSON for Metrics Analyst
ux_changes: Tableau workbook/dashboard/sheet browser with drill-down navigation and field detail sidebar.
middleware_changes: Tableau GraphQL/REST API integration for workbook hierarchy, views, and field metadata.
data_model_changes: Storage for Tableau workbook views, sheets, and field metadata with usage data.
api_changes: Tableau GraphQL and REST API queries for workbook hierarchy and usage data.
implementation_notes: GraphQL and REST API may both be needed. __typename distinguishes dashboards from sheets. path indicates end-user accessibility.
comment_summary: Developer requested a meeting to discuss the scope and implementation details.
